docs.title: Digital Transformation Center Makerspace Documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to the Digital Transformation Center's Makerspace Documentation site. This site provides documentation, guides, and more resources for the DTC's Makerspace capabilities.

This site is currently under development, so check back soon for updates.

docs.title: Available Documentation
docs.text: • Universal Robots UR5e

docs.title: Robotics Home
docs.text: The DTC supports educational and early-stage research and development through robotic platforms. If you would like to host a demo, teach an audience, or prove your robotic project concept, the DTC is here to help.

docs.title: Platforms
docs.text:
The Digital Transformation Center currently has the following robotic equipment available for use.

  • Universal Robots UR5e

docs.title: UR5e Home
docs.text: The Universal Robots UR5e is a lightweight, 6-axis collaborative robotic arm (cobot) designed to work safely alongside humans.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Usage Log: All activity on the UR5e must be logged. Follow this link to update the log.
  • Machine Calendar: Book time with the UR5e or see when it's available. Users with reservations will have priority access.

docs.title: References
docs.text: • User Manual

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Before using the UR5e, you must complete hands-on training. Schedule time here or contact Ryan Kuederle.

After completing training, you can reference the UR5e Standard Operating Procedures during regular use. Guides for advanced features will be made available in the future.

docs.title: Pre-Start Checklist
docs.text:
• Completed hands-on operational training.
  • Cleared workspace and ensured UR5e is safe from colliding with surroudings.
  • Ensured tool installation is secure.
  • Detatched the teach pendant and moved away from the robot.
  • Deactivated emergency-stop switch.
Warning: Do not start the robot before all pre-start checklist items are completed.

Warning

Do not move the UR5e from its current location. Doing so risks damage to property.
Starting the Robot

Power on the UR5e by pressing and releasing the power button on the front of the teach pendant. The button should glow green and the system will boot to the home screen.

On the teach pendant, press the power control button in the bottom left, which should indicate that the robot is powered off.

Before powering on the robot, be sure the correct payload is selected. This selection affects how the robot counteracts the payload mass during operation. Once the payload is confirmed, press the ON button to power on the robot.

After the robot is powered on and initialized, ensure the workspace around it is clear from anything the robot might collide with. Then, press the START button. The brakes will audibly release, and the robot is ready for operation. After the robot starts, press the Exit button to return home.

Basic Controls Programming Robot Shutdown

docs.title: Manual Manipulation
docs.text: To manipulate the robot with your hands, hold the Freedrive button located on the back of the pendant. While this button is depressed, you can manually manipulate the robot's joints. Before entering Freedrive, be sure the active payload is accurate and is not holding any additional mass. Payload calibration is especially important when using Freedrive.

docs.title: Moving with the Pendant
docs.text:
To move the robot using the pendant, navigate to the Move menu by clicking the Move icon in the top left of the screen. To move the tool head, use the arrow controls on the left-hand side of the control in terface. The clickable arrows correspond to one of three axes:

  • x-axis: indicated by a red arrow, controlled by forward/backward arrows.
  • y-axis: indicated by a green arrow, controlled by left/right arrows.
  • z-axis: indicated by a blue arrow, controlled by up/down arrows.

The Feature by which control axes are oriented can be changed through the Features dropdown. For example, changing the Feature to Base will orient the control axes with respect to the base of the arm, often providing a more intuitive control frame of reference.

docs.title: Programming
docs.text: Universal Robots offers five ways to program the UR5e. When programming the UR5e, the UR Developer Page is your friend.

docs.title: 1. Visual Programming
docs.text: The easiest and most flexible way to program the UR5e is visual programming. This method is great for simple repetitive tasks. To use the visual programmer, navigate to the Program screen by selecting the Program button in the upper left-hand corner of the screen. This menu allows the user to select various program components to assemble simple actions.

docs.title: 2. URScript
docs.text: URScript acts as a robust programming language for controlling Universal Robot arms. Every visual program is translated to a URScript before being executed. This allows the user to add script to their program either on the teach pendant or from a computer. Learn more about using URScript to program this robot here.

docs.title: 3. URCaps
docs.text:
URCaps act as apps or plugins enabling developers to create their own URCap for others to use. URCaps can contain program nodes, installation settings, tool bars, and other custom user interface components. A URCap can encompass both visual components and robot control through URScript. Background processes can also be included in URCaps, typically used to communicate with external hardware and to interact with controls while the robot is working.

Learn more about developing URCaps here.

docs.title: 4. External Control
docs.text:
While the previous methods are designed to run on the robot with no additional hardware, the robot also allows for external control. External control allows for things like monitoring and controling registers; loading, starting, and stopping programs; and externally controlling motion.

Learn more about the external control URCap here.

docs.title: 5. ROS
docs.text:
Robot Operating System (ROS) is a powerful, industry-standard tool for programming robots. It offers precise control and powerful building blocks for developing custom robot software. ROS is a special case of External Control, but gets its own category due to its wide use and support. Universal Robots provides a ROS 2 driver which receives regular updates.

Check out our example for running ROS2 and MoveIt 2 with the Universal Robots UR5e here.

External control of the UR5e with ROS requires an External Control program to be running on the robot. To create such a program, in the Program screen, select "External Control" from the URCaps dropdown. Then, press the play button in the bottom menu to execute the program.

docs.title: Robot Shutdown
docs.text: To shutdown the robot, select the hamburger menu in the top right corner of the screen, then select Shutdown Robot. Then, select Power off. You may be asked to save your configuration. Note that it is your responsibility to remove any saved sensitive data from the robot.

docs.title: Post-Shutdown Checklist
docs.text:
• The robot is left in an appropriate resting position. The arm should be folded to be compact and not protruding into any common walkways.
  • The pendant is hung in its proper position on the front of the control cabinet.
  • Any cords, such as power cords, ethernet cables, or the pendant connection should be wound and neatly stowed.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The UR5e is a 6-axis collaborative robot arm (cobot) from Universal Robots' e-Series designed for medium-duty tasks that require flexibility, ease of programming, and safe human-robot collaboration. It is commonly used for assembly, pick & place, machine tending, testing, and research applications.

docs.title: Key Specifications
docs.text:
• Payload: 5 kg
  • Reach (max): 850 mm
  • Degrees of freedom: 6 (6 joints)
  • Repeatability: ±0.03 mm
  • Robot mass: ~20.6 kg
  • Mounting: Floor, ceiling, wall, or angled (standard flange)
  • Protection class: IP54 (robot arm)

docs.title: Mechanical
docs.text:
• Joints / Range of motion: 6 rotary joints; full joint ranges configurable in Polyscope (see official datasheet for per-joint limits).
  • Flange: Standard ISO mounting flange for tool mounting and external connectors.
  • Cable routing: External and partially internal options (specific routing depends on mounting orientation and tooling).

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
• Typical cycle times: Application dependent; UR5e optimized for quick point-to-point moves within its workspace.
  • Motion control: Smooth, force-aware motion with built-in collision detection and joint-level safety limits.

docs.title: Electrical & Power
docs.text:
• Power supply: Controller cabinet supplies robot arm; requires standard 120VAC 10A circuit.
  • Peripheral Power Delivery Power accessible at various voltage levels from controller.

docs.title: Communication & I/O
docs.text:
• Standard interfaces: Ethernet (TCP/IP), real-time Ethernet options, digital and analog I/O via controller.
  • Fieldbus support: Via UR+ certified modules and I/O interfaces (EtherCAT, PROFINET, DeviceNet, etc.).

docs.title: Software & Programming
docs.text:
• Programming environment: Polyscope GUI (teach pendant) with drag-and-drop programming and scriptable URScript.
  • Ecosystem: URCaps for third-party integrations; supports real-time control and ROS interoperability.

docs.title: Safety & Compliance
docs.text:
• Safety features: Built-in force/torque sensing for collision detection, configurable safety limits, and safe stop functions when used with the UR controller.
  • Additional Features: A safety laser is installed and can be configured for proximity detection.

docs.title: Mounting & Installation
docs.text:
• Mounting orientations: Floor, ceiling, wall, or custom fixtures using the standard wrist/flange. DTC UR5e mounted on rolling cart for flexibility
  • Environmental considerations: IP54 on arm; keep controller and electrical components in appropriate enclosures and follow temperature/humidity requirements from official documentation.

docs.title: Accessories & Options
docs.text:
• End effectors: Grippers, vacuum systems, sensors, custom tooling.
  • Controller options: e-Series teach pendant and controller cabinet. Additional UR+ hardware modules available for extended functionality.

docs.title: Notes & References
docs.text: For full, definitive specifications (joint ranges, detailed electrical specs, mounting dimensions, and environmental ratings) consult the official Universal Robots UR5e datasheet and user manual.
